};

const tmpPath = '/tmp/gh-aw/aw_info.json';
fs.writeFileSync(tmpPath, JSON.stringify(awInfo, null, 2));
console.log('Generated aw_info.json at:', tmpPath);
console.log(JSON.stringify(awInfo, null, 2));

core.setOutput('model', awInfo.model);

2026-03-23T09:27:16.2536288Z 
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2536441Z ## Summary of Fixes
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2536656Z 
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2537130Z ### 1. `documentation/MIGRATION-guide-for-next-generation-management-libraries.md`
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2538495Z - **Typo**: `clientSecrat` → `clientSecret` in the authentication migration code example
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2539115Z 
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2539372Z ### 2. `documentation/resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md`
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2541288Z - **Broken shell command**: Missing `/` path separator in the `git add` command — `` `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml `` would produce an invalid path; fixed to `` `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml ``
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2542715Z 
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2542982Z ### 3. `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2544857Z - **Outdated test framework**: Both code examples used mocha/chai patterns (`import { assert } from "chai"`, `import { Context } from "mocha"`, `this.currentTest`) but the repository has migrated to **vitest**. Updated to:
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2546528Z   - Use `import { assert, ... } from "vitest"` instead of chai
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2547391Z   - Use `import type { TestInfo } from "@azure-tools/test-recorder"` instead of mocha `Context`
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2549009Z   - Use `beforeEach(async (ctx) => { recorder = new Recorder(ctx) })` (vitest style) instead of `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) { recorder = new Recorder(this.currentTest) })` (mocha style)
2026-03-23T09:27:16.2586317Z   - Use arrow functions throughout, and `.js` extensions in imports (ESM)

2026-03-23T09:27:33.0752944Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-23T09:27:06.798221914Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"This PR fixes several documentation inconsistencies found in the `documentation/` folder.\n\n## Changes\n\n### `documentation/MIGRATION-guide-for-next-generation-management-libraries.md`\n\n- **Typo fix**: `clientSecrat` → `clientSecret` in the code example showing how to migrate from `@azure/ms-rest-nodeauth` to `@azure/identity`. The variable `clientSecret` is defined two lines above and was referenced with a typo.\n\n### `documentation/resolve-pnpm-lock-merge-conflict.md`\n\n- **Missing path separator fix**: The combined shell command for resolving pnpm-lock merge conflicts was missing a `/` between the `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` subshell and `pnpm-lock.yaml` in the final `git add` step. This would result in an invalid path like `/home/user/repos/azure-sdk-for-jspnpm-lock.yaml` instead of `/home/user/repos/azure-sdk-for-js/pnpm-lock.yaml`.\n\n  Before: `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`pnpm-lock.yaml ``\n  \n  After: `` git add `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`/pnpm-lock.yaml ``\n\n### `documentation/Quickstart-on-how-to-write-tests.md`\n\nThe test code examples in this file were using mocha/chai patterns which are outdated — the repository uses **vitest** as its test framework. Updated both example files:\n\n**`glossary.spec.ts` example:**\n- Replaced `import { assert } from \"chai\"` with `import { assert, beforeEach, afterEach, describe, it } from \"vitest\"`\n- Replaced `import { PurviewDataMapClient } from \"../../src\"` with proper ESM import using `.js` extension\n- Replaced mocha-style `beforeEach(async function () { recorder = await createRecorder(this) }` with vitest arrow function style `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e { recorder = await createRecorder(ctx) })`\n- Replaced `afterEach(async function () {` with `afterEach(async () =\u003e {`\n\n**`utils/recordedClient.ts` example:**\n- Removed `import { Context } from \"mocha\"` (mocha is not used in this repo)\n- Added `import type { TestInfo } from \"@azure-tools/test-recorder\"` — `TestInfo` is the vitest-compatible type the recorder expects\n- Changed `createRecorder(context: Context)` to `createRecorder(context: TestInfo)`\n- Changed `new Recorder(context.currentTest)` to `new Recorder(context)` — the recorder now takes a vitest `TestInfo` (context) directly, not `context.currentTest`\n- Updated imports to use `.js` ESM extensions\n- Changed `import { ClientOptions }` to `import type { ClientOptions }` (type-only import)\n\n**`monitor.spec.ts` example:**\n- Replaced `import { assert } from \"chai\"` with vitest imports\n- Removed `import { Context } from \"mocha\"`\n- Updated `import { MonitorClient } from \"../src/monitorClient\"` to use `.js` extension\n- Changed `beforeEach(async function (this: Context) { recorder = new Recorder(this.currentTest) }` to `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e { recorder = new Recorder(ctx) })`\n- Changed all remaining `async function` callbacks to arrow functions\n\n## Verification\n\nThe correct vitest-style patterns were verified against actual test files in the repository:\n- `sdk/purview/purview-datamap-rest/test/public/utils/recordedClient.ts` — uses `TestInfo` from `@azure-tools/test-recorder` and `new Recorder(context)` \n- `sdk/monitor/arm-monitor/test/monitor.spec.ts` — uses `beforeEach(async (ctx) =\u003e { recorder = new Recorder(ctx) }` and imports from vitest\n","branch":"docs/fix-documentation-inconsistencies","title":"docs: fix documentation inconsistencies"},"name":"create_pull_request"}}}

2026-03-23T09:27:34.8098154Z ##[warning]Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/download-artifact@018cc2cf5baa6db3ef3c5f8a56943fffe632ef53. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 2nd, 2026. Please check if updated versions of these actions are available that support Node.js 24. To opt into Node.js 24 now, set the FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true environment variable on the runner or in your workflow file. Once Node.js 24 becomes the default, you can temporarily opt out by setting ACTIONS_ALLOW_USE_UNSECURE_NODE_VERSION=true. For more information see: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/
